edges.desc: serves as the characters' home base during the adventure, where they can rest and get supplies
established and leads the cloister of Dragon's Rest as a safe haven from violence
lives on Stormwreck Isle and is deeply connected to its history and magical scars
has a secret lair on Stormwreck Isle where she resides
the adventure begins with Runara's sanctuary being threatened by the dragon conflict
Runara killed a blue dragon at the Clifftop Observatory
The characters explore and experience events on Stormwreck Isle
Characters explore Seagrow Caves in Chapter 2
Characters investigate the cursed shipwreck in Chapter 3
Characters are brought to the Clifftop Observatory in Chapter 4
The characters arrive at locations marked on the map of Stormwreck Isle
encountered by players in the sea north of Stormwreck Isle
traveling by ship across the trackless sea
intimidates and extorts the players during their voyage
Players are currently in the sea north of Stormwreck Isle as a storm approaches
is present in the ship's hold demanding tribute
demands payment of 400 gp tribute to the Scaled Queen from the players
players will attack the Merrow Extortionist first, with characters acting in combat order
The characters seek out Runara's wisdom and assistance regarding the dangers facing Stormwreck Isle
The player's ship makes its way toward the calm harbor on the island's north side
The sailors set the player ashore on the rickety dock
The player admires the towering statue at the center of the temple
The player is at the base of the long path that winds up the side of the cliff
The player's visit to Dragon's Rest begins
shambling sailors who died in a recent shipwreck at this location
zombies move toward characters and make melee attacks if characters choose to fight
The players are present on the beach, about to leave when they encounter the drowned sailors
wake up in the temple (area A5 in Dragon's Rest) after being rescued
comes to their rescue and prevents zombies from dragging them into the sea
Elder Runara is the leader of Dragon's Rest and welcomes visitors to the cloister
Myla sells equipment from the Basic Rules to the characters during their stay at Dragon's Rest
Characters can sleep in the monastic cells (area A1) during their stay at Dragon's Rest
An elderly human woman welcomes the players to Dragon's Rest and offers Bahamut's guidance for their quest
Nine kobolds are utterly devoted to Runara and live at Dragon's Rest
Nine kobolds live at Dragon's Rest and work there at night
Kobolds share Runara's ideals of justice and compassion, being lawful good
Myla is a kobold tinkerer who lives at Dragon's Rest with the other kobolds
Runara helped Myla recover from wing injuries and Myla now lives at Dragon's Rest
Myla's wings were badly injured in an attack by Stirge, which she describes negatively
Myla's brothers Mek and Minn now follow Sparkrender, the Blue Dragon Wyrmling
Kobolds are initially friendly toward adventurers unless otherwise noted
Tarak tends the cloister's garden plots, growing flowers, herbs, and vegetables as an avid botanist
Tarak frequently visits the sea caves on the south side of the island to acquire heart cap mushrooms from the myconids
Tarak visits the myconid caves to acquire heart cap mushrooms from the myconids that live there
Tarak uses heart cap mushrooms acquired from myconids to make Potions of Healing
Tarak is worried about being turned away from the sea caves by a fungus-covered octopus monster and may give quests to players (see 'Cloister Quests')
spends her twilight years in reflective contemplation at Dragon's Rest
uses mason's tools to maintain the temple and other areas of the cloister
can shift her attitude to friendly by engaging in conversation on history, ethics, and the impact of individual actions
well-tended garden plots are located along the lower part of the cliffside path
the path widens into a long plaza about thirty feet above the bay
a stone statue of a dragon is located halfway along the plaza
six open doorways are cut into the cliff side along the plaza
tells characters the statue depicts Astalagan, who died on these cliffs centuries ago
doesn't tell characters that Astalagan was her father, keeping this information secret
occupies the second cell from the west
occupies the third cell from the west
occupies the fourth cell, which is cluttered with junk and tools
can occupy the vacant westernmost cell if they don't mind sharing
characters can operate the winch inside the building to lower or raise the pallet
cooks meals in the kitchen on certain days
share three daily meals in the kitchen with cloister residents
spends nearly half her time in the library studying, copying, and annotating the collection
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
comes to the library to read and discuss various works
visits the library and is described as studious among the kobolds
Players enter the library through the oak door and explore the space
spends about half her time here, tending and maintaining the temple, offering prayers and incense, or in quiet contemplation
helps Runara maintain the temple along with other residents of the cloister
Adventurers pray to Bahamut at the temple to uphold honor and justice or for courage against threats
zombies have attacked Blepp the kobold, leaving him with only 2 hit points
zombies are overpowering Tarak the human gardener who is unarmed
the lower path connects the coastal area to Dragon's Rest
characters can defeat zombies when they first arrive on the island
is eager to reestablish contact with the myconids of the sea caves
witnessed a ship crash on the rocks north of Dragon's Rest
suggests the answer to the recent crashes can be found at the older wreck of Compass Rose
Runara is present at the cloister where she interacts with the character
recommends the paladin speak with Tarak about escaping corruption
recommends the paladin speak with Varnoth about escaping violence
Tarak is currently at Dragon's Rest, having escaped there after his lover was killed
fears Aidron went to the observatory and suspects evil has arisen there, but dares not go herself
three Fume Drakes are the guardians of the spring, initially hidden in the water
guardians of the spring who protect the mushrooms and water from intruders
becomes hostile and emerges from the water to attack if players gather mushrooms or enter the water
The players have arrived at and are exploring the luminescent cove with its hot spring and glowing water
hostile toward characters, views them as intruders in its territory and tries to drive them away
stranded on the island after shipwreck, now considers this area its territory
stranded on the island after shipwreck, now considers this area its territory
Four kobolds and one winged kobold participate together in the ambush as a group
These kobolds reject the peaceful teachings of Runara
Kobolds are hiding in rocks and light foliage around the island wilderness
Kobolds ambush travelers on the cliffside path, preying on those who stray from Dragon's Rest
Kobolds attempt to ambush the player characters, fighting to the death unless offered money or food
angry kobolds emerge from hiding places and attack the players in the dormitory
angry kobolds emerge from hiding places and attack the players in the warrens
was formed from magic-fueled volcanic activity in the tomb of Sharruth
suggests that all is not well beneath the island
characters might investigate Sharruth's tomb as an adventure
characters access winding tunnels leading deep into the earth
a group of kobolds might serve mighty Sharruth
leader of the myconid colony in the caves
noxious fumes from the dragon's tomb are poisoning the myconids and twisting their gardens
leader of the myconids who is currently laid low by the blight
stirges nest in the caves and can be deadly in large numbers to visitors
The rowboat is located at Dragon's Rest and can be used to travel to Seagrow Caves
Tarak prefers the rowboat method for traveling to Seagrow Caves
Players are positioned on the cliffside path overlooking the opening below
adult myconids live in the Seagrow Caves and use rapport spores to warn visitors to leave
myconid sprouts live in the Seagrow Caves and flee to warn adults of intruders
sprouts flee toward the nearest adults to warn them of intruders
Sinensa is the leader of the myconids in Seagrow Caves
Sinensa is the myconid leader who has fallen ill in the Seagrow Caves
mentioning Tarak's name or presenting his offering grants advantage on Charisma checks with myconids
myconids defend themselves if the characters attack them first
characters can attack the myconids, who will then defend themselves
characters are present in the myconid caves where Rapport Spores are active
lurks in the water of the entrance tunnel, attacking non-myconid creatures
attacks any creatures (other than myconids) who enter the tunnel, regardless of tide state
The players are present in the entrance tunnel, observing the bioluminescent fungus and swirling spores in the water
two Stirge clinging to the tunnel ceiling in the entrance tunnel
if fighting breaks out in the area, the disturbed stirges join the battle against the players
Molen and Kraz are spreading fertilizer from area B3 in the fungal cavern
Hipsiz and Rugoso tend mushrooms near the upper pool in the fungal cavern
Three violet fungus grow among the harmless mushrooms in the fungal cavern
If characters move into the cave toward the myconids, one violet fungus attacks them with rot-causing tendrils
The players advance into the fungal cavern fungus farm
characters kill violet fungi with ranged attacks while staying out of their reach
The Myconid Sprout avoids the characters and the violet fungi
The Myconid Adult moves quickly to protect the sprouts when aware of danger through combat noise or Distress Spores
After characters defeat the violet fungi, the myconids' attitude improves to indifferent and they are willing to speak using Rapport Spores
The adults agree to accompany the characters and vouch for them with the rest of the myconid colony
A character examines the fungi in the cavern and notices many mushrooms are sickly, shriveled, and blotched with black patches of decay
can make heart cap mushrooms into potions of healing
Three named sprouts (Bispo, Valup, Popple) work here gathering fertilizer for area B2
Six stirges live in a nest in the chamber and will attack intruders
Sprouts rely on stirges as a distraction to escape from intruders
Agric and Omphalo are tending to and caring for the other four myconids (Craterel, Pleuro, Subrufus, and Virosa) who are in a meld trance
The players are present in the fungal grotto cavern
Auranta and Enok are tending to the unconscious Sinensa, collecting spores from a glowing red fungus to keep their leader alive
Two Myconid Adults named Auranta and Enok are present in the sanctum, tending to their leader
Tarak is located in the cloister cells where he creates elixirs
Tarak creates and possesses the elixir of health in the cloister
Two Fume Drakes lurk amid the sulfurous fumes in this cave
This cave is the source of the blight spreading through the myconid colony
The heart of the problem is the tomb of the red dragon Sharruth deep beneath the island, which causes volcanic activity producing noxious fumes
The dragon's energy tears open rifts to the Elemental Plane of Fire, which is the origin of the Fume Drakes
released from a stony egg when the orange crystal is broken, now present in the cave
sees the characters only as fuel and attacks them at once upon being released
hatches from its egg in the cave
Sinensa gives the ruby morel and permission to keep treasure to the players when they awaken
Tarak creates and possesses the elixir of health at Dragon's Rest
characters may visit Seagrow Caves before the wreck of Compass Rose as part of their adventure progression
characters are urged to visit the wreck of Compass Rose next after Seagrow Caves, or may have already explored it in chapter 3
characters are ready to visit Clifftop Observatory in chapter 4 after reaching 3rd level
settled into the wreck of Compass Rose
lures ships to crash on the shoal with its magically entrancing voice
feasts on unlucky sailors who crash on the shoal
shambling zombies from shipwrecks menace the island's shore
some zombies have been trapped in the wreck for years
characters can decide to explore other recent wrecks in the sunken fleet
giant octopuses (using spore servant octopus stat block) might be found aboard other wrecks
Ghoul might be found aboard other wrecks as a danger
Dragon's Rest has a rowboat the characters can take
characters can pull the rowboat up to the south side of the ship and tie it to the rigging
characters can take the rowboat to visit the wreck, requiring about 1 hour and 40 minutes to row 2½ miles
characters can climb onto the main deck (area C1) after tying up the rowboat
The players are exploring the main deck of the wrecked ship
characters climb the rope ladder up the mast to reach the crow's nest
returns to the shipwreck's main deck after looking for other ships to lure onto rocks
two small tiger eye gems found in the crow's nest
one bloodstone gem found in the crow's nest
characters search through the crow's nest
is located in area C4 on the quarterdeck and responds to loud noises
two zombies are inside the captain's quarters, either beside the door or shuffling around the cabin
Players have entered the captain's quarters and are exploring the ruined cabin
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
found in one of the two small drawers in the desk
The players are currently in the galley, observing the scene
The portrait shows a blond-haired man wearing merchant's fine clothes, which the players can observe in the crew quarters
Characters are searching the cabin in the crew quarters
rests in a small compartment beneath the raised floorboard in the crew quarters
is an obvious threat in the lower deck
lurks in the aft part of the hold, waiting to attack
waits to attack characters when they're busy with the zombie, hoping to paralyze and drag one away
is more cunning than the zombies and uses them as a distraction for its own attacks
The players have descended to the lower deck and are exploring the area
can be used by characters to pry open crates more efficiently
found in a crate containing 20 pounds of whole cloves worth 60 gp
found in a crate, a fine lute with mother-of-pearl inlay worth 50 gp
found in a crate, a spell scroll of command sealed in a leather case
The characters are swimming around in the submerged hold, able to surface for air as needed
contained within the iron chest in the ship's hold
three turquoise gems are inside the pouch
contained within the iron chest in the ship's hold
The player is in the northern shoals area where the Compass Rose wrecked
Many Undead creatures like Ghoul worship Orcus, the Demon Prince of Undeath
makes its lair in the crow's nest (area C1)
confronts the characters on the main deck when they show themselves, using Luring Song on its first turn
protects treasure stored in the crow's nest
can reduce the harpy to fewer than half its hit points, potentially causing it to flee
can negotiate with the harpy to leave in exchange for returning its treasure from the crow's nest
Runara knows about Brastos being laid to rest in the clifftop cemetery and directs players there
Runara can warn the characters that they're not ready to face this part of the adventure yet if they come to the observatory too early
battled her blue dragon rival centuries ago, wrecking the observatory
has become the home of an arrogant blue dragon wyrmling called Sparkrender
overpowered Aidron and imprisoned him in the observatory ruins
imprisoned in the observatory ruins by Sparkrender
the two wyrmlings met and immediately clashed
Aidron's hatred of chromatic dragons was stronger than his disagreement with Runara
The characters approach the observatory by land, eventually arriving at area D1
The characters see Sparkrender come to rest on a rocky spire off the coast
A character examines the crystalline protrusions or scarred vegetation, recognizing signs of lightning strikes or dragon breath
Players are present at the coastal cliffs, considering options to access the ruins
kobolds will attack players if they don't leave after being warned
kobolds can be convinced to allow exploration with a successful Charisma check
Two winged kobolds are locked in battle with eight stirges at the observatory ruins
If characters help the kobolds during battle, the kobolds return the favor and become friendly
If characters attack the kobolds instead of or as well as the stirges, the kobolds fight back
The kobolds (Mek and Minn) left their sister Myla for dead after a stirge attack that maimed her wings
The winged kobolds are present at the observatory ruins, fighting stirges
Eight stirges are attacking at the observatory ruins
If friendly, the kobolds offer to help retrieve the moonstone key from area D1 to activate the bridge to D4
can create a bridge to the overlook by inserting the moonstone key in the western dragon statue
can create a bridge to the crumbled tower by inserting the moonstone key in the southwestern dragon statue
Three kobolds named Ekrash, Erp, and Hev inhabit the camp in the kobold warrens
Two winged kobolds named Nuhro and Snirke inhabit the camp in the kobold warrens
Kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
Winged kobolds are polishing sling bullets and keeping busy until Sparkrender's ritual
Characters are present in the isolated study location where they need to cross a 22-foot gap
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
found in a hidden compartment behind a loose brick in the northwest wall
the potion is contained within the pouch along with 10 gp
Sparkrender the blue dragon wyrmling is currently sleeping here, curled up against the northeast wall
The players can free Bronze Dragon Wyrmling (Aidron) from the secret library below, and then fight Sparkrender with Aidron's help
The players can waken the dragon and fight him now, or they can sneak past the dragon and fight him later with or without Aidron's help
Sparkrender is hostile toward the characters and will attack them if not convinced otherwise
Sparkrender becomes aggressive if characters mention Runara's name
the secret archive is hidden beneath the observatory tower and accessed via a spiral staircase
five pale blue quartz crystals are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the tower
five blue jasper stones are part of Sparkrender's hoard in the tower
characters can gather Sparkrender's hoard in the celestial observatory tower
trapped inside the secret archive by Sparkrender after being weakened and forced in
Aidron asks for help defeating Sparkrender and expresses concern for island inhabitants
found by searching the library with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check
found by searching the library with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check
described in appendix A as being in the library
described in appendix A as being in the library
Sparkrender (blue dragon wyrmling) is performing a ritual at the clifftop observatory
Aidron (bronze dragon wyrmling) is imprisoned in area D6 of the observatory
Runara gives each character a potion of healing as a reward
Runara gives each character an exquisite pearl worth 100 gp as a reward
